skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render React and Remotion TSX components into MP4 videos through the inference.sh belt CLI.
skill.summary: Turn Remotion component code into rendered video files using inference.sh.
skill.author: inference-skills

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many shell and weak-crypto patterns, but the weak-crypto alerts are false positives on markdown text. The confirmed risk is legitimate but elevated: the skill permits belt CLI execution and sends user-provided Remotion code and props to inference.sh for rendering.

medium_findings.title: Broad belt CLI Execution Permission
medium_findings.description: The skill declares allowed-tools as Bash(belt *), which permits running any belt subcommand. This is necessary for the documented renderer, but it is broader than a single fixed command and should be reviewed before use in sensitive workspaces.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The permission is explicit in the skill front matter. The risk is capability scope rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: User Code Sent to External Rendering Service
medium_findings.description: The examples pass user-supplied TSX code and props to infsh/remotion-render through the belt CLI. This matches the skill purpose, but private source text, data, or media references may leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented inputs clearly include TSX code and props submitted to the inference.sh app. This is intended functionality but creates privacy and supply-chain exposure.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Documentation and Image URLs
low_findings.description: The skill contains links to inference.sh, cloud.inference.sh, GitHub raw content, and Remotion documentation. These are normal references, but clients may fetch external content when displaying or following the links.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are directly visible and are consistent with documentation and preview media. I found no evidence of credential exfiltration in these links.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md lines 3 and 33. Those lines contain the skill description and a markdown table header, with no cryptographic operation or dependency.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines were reviewed and do not contain cryptographic code. The alert appears to be a pattern collision in markdown text.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill repeatedly demonstrates belt and npx commands. These commands are expected for this skill, but they execute local CLI tools and may contact external services.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are explicit in fenced examples. They are legitimate usage examples, not hidden execution logic.

content.value_statement: Rendering Remotion videos from code can require setup, dependencies, and export configuration. This skill sends TSX component code to inference.sh and returns rendered MP4 output.
actual_capabilities: Runs the inference.sh remotion-render app with TSX component input.
Supports common Remotion APIs such as AbsoluteFill, Sequence, spring, and interpolate.
Accepts render settings for width, height, FPS, duration, and codec.
Passes component props into the rendered Remotion composition.
Shows CLI, Python SDK, and streaming progress usage patterns.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh belt CLI or Python SDK account setup.
Sends render inputs to an external service, so sensitive code needs review first.
Only documented imports from Remotion and React are described.
Rendering cost, queue time, and output availability depend on the external service.

use_cases.title: Render Marketing Motion Graphics
use_cases.description: Create short branded MP4 animations from Remotion components without maintaining a local render stack.

use_cases.title: Automate Data-Driven Videos
use_cases.description: Generate videos that use props for titles, counters, metrics, or other dynamic values.

use_cases.title: Prototype React Video Ideas
use_cases.description: Test Remotion animation concepts quickly with configurable duration, frame rate, and resolution.

prompt_templates.title: Render a Simple Text Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple Remotion component with fading title text, then render it as a 1080p MP4.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Social Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a square Remotion video with animated headline, subtitle, and progress movement for a social post.

prompt_templates.title: Use Props for Variants
prompt_templates.prompt: Render one Remotion component with props for title, subtitle, colors, and duration so I can reuse it for multiple variants.

prompt_templates.title: Render a Sequenced Explainer
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Remotion composition with timed sequences, spring transitions, and configured output settings for a short explainer video.

output_examples.input: A request to render a three-second fading title animation.
output_examples.output: An MP4 video URL or file reference for the completed Remotion render.

output_examples.input: A square animated counter video with custom duration and frame rate.
output_examples.output: A rendered square MP4 suitable for social or product updates.

output_examples.input: A component that receives title and subtitle props.
output_examples.output: A rendered video variant using the supplied text values.

best_practices: Keep submitted TSX focused on the render and avoid embedding secrets or private source code.
Set width, height, FPS, and duration explicitly for repeatable output.
Use props for text and data values instead of editing the component for each variant.
anti_patterns: Do not submit confidential business data unless external rendering is approved.
Do not rely on undocumented packages or imports in the TSX component.
Do not leave render settings implicit when the output must match a delivery spec.

faq.question: What does this skill render?
faq.answer: It renders React and Remotion TSX component code into MP4 video output through inference.sh.

faq.question: Does it require a local Remotion setup?
faq.answer: The documented workflow uses the inference.sh belt CLI or Python SDK instead of a local renderer.

faq.question: Can I control video size and frame rate?
faq.answer: Yes. The input schema includes width, height, FPS, duration, and codec options.

faq.question: Can the component receive props?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents a props object that is passed into the default component.

faq.question: Is this safe for private code?
faq.answer: Use caution. The render inputs are sent to an external service, so sensitive code should be reviewed first.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
